ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general roasted chicken food product entry; no specific half-portion Cantonese roast chicken entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers cooked chicken broadly; no specific roasted half-chicken entry exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 covers roast chicken generically; no half-portion specific code exists.

serving.description: Half roasted chicken (approximately 400–500 g edible portion)
serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is half a roasted chicken, approximately 450 g, commonly shared or consumed as a main course in a Chinese meal.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken, broilers or fryers, roasted, meat and skin — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 05013)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for roasted chicken (meat and skin, broiler/fryer) used as the primary nutrient basis. Values represent the whole roasted bird per 100 g edible portion. Cantonese-style marinade (soy, five-spice, sesame) adds modest sodium; sodium value adjusted upward to approximately 350 mg per 100 g to reflect typical soy-based marinade contribution, consistent with HPB Singapore food composition data for Chinese roast chicken. All other macros retained from USDA SR Legacy roasted chicken (meat and skin).

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pure roasted chicken contains negligible digestible carbohydrate (0 g per 100 g). Meat and poultry products with zero carbohydrate content are universally considered to have no glycemic response. Per Veritas v2 critical rule for zero-carbohydrate foods, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive value) and gi_category to 'low' by convention. This aligns with the principle that such foods do not elicit a glycemic response, as supported by the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed GI literature (Atkinson et al. 2008). glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g is 0.

hypertension.guidance: Half roasted chicken prepared with soy sauce marinade contributes approximately 350 mg sodium per 100 g; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 150–200 g and request reduced-sodium preparation where possible to keep total meal sodium within the recommended 2000 mg per day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate content and high protein
type2_diabetes.guidance: Half roasted chicken contains virtually no digestible carbohydrate and has a glycemic index of 1 (effectively zero), making it a suitable high-protein, low-glycemic main dish for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; portion control remains important to manage total caloric intake.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and cholesterol from skin-on roasted chicken
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Skin-on roasted chicken provides 3.8 g saturated fat and approximately 88 mg cholesterol per 100 g; individuals with cardiovascular disease are advised to remove the skin before consumption and limit serving size to 150 g to reduce saturated fat intake in line with AHA dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Half Roasted Chicken (烧鸡半只, Shāo jī bàn zhī) is a classic Cantonese-style roasted poultry dish widely available in Chinese roast-meat restaurants (烧腊店) across Singapore, Hong Kong, and mainland China. The dish consists of half a whole chicken — typically including the breast, thigh, drumstick, and wing on one side — that has been marinated in a blend of soy sauce, five-spice powder, garlic, ginger, sesame oil, and a maltose or honey glaze before being roasted at high heat until the skin is deeply golden, lacquered, and crisp. The flesh is tender, juicy, and richly savoury with subtle sweetness from the glaze. Nutritionally, it is a high-protein, moderate-fat food providing approximately 239 kcal, 27.3 g protein, and 13.6 g fat per 100 g. Carbohydrate content is negligible (0 g), giving it a glycemic index of 1 (effectively zero) and making it suitable for low-carbohydrate and diabetic dietary patterns. Sodium content is elevated (~350 mg per 100 g) due to the soy-based marinade, which is a consideration for individuals managing hypertension. The skin contributes saturated fat (3.8 g per 100 g) and cholesterol (~88 mg per 100 g), relevant for cardiovascular disease management. In Singapore, half roasted chicken is commonly served with steamed rice, cucumber slices, and a dipping sauce of ginger-scallion oil or dark soy sauce. It is a popular hawker centre and restaurant main course, often ordered as part of a shared Chinese meal.
common_user_queries: How many calories in half roasted chicken?
Is roasted chicken good for diabetics?
Nutrition facts for Chinese roast chicken
How much protein in half a roasted chicken?
Is siu gai high in sodium?
Can I eat roast chicken if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy roasted chicken (meat and skin), which is a well-validated primary source for roasted poultry. Sodium has been adjusted upward to reflect typical Cantonese soy-based marinade contribution, consistent with HPB Singapore food composition data for Chinese roast chicken. No exact HPB Singapore entry for half roasted chicken with full nutrient panel was available at time of processing; hence confidence is medium rather than high.
notes: 1. carbohydrate_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for roasted chicken meat and skin — roasted chicken contains no digestible carbohydrate. 2. sugar_g is 0.0: confirmed zero; the maltose/honey glaze contributes negligible residual sugar to the cooked product per 100 g. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: naturally zero — animal muscle tissue contains no dietary fibre. 4. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for roasted chicken. 5. glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) for this zero-carbohydrate animal protein food, as per Veritas v2 critical rule for zero-carbohydrate foods; gi_category is 'low' per convention for foods with no glycemic carbohydrate, consistent with University of Sydney GI Database classification of pure meat/poultry. 6. trans_fat_g of 0.1 g per 100 g reflects naturally occurring trans fats (vaccenic acid) present in poultry at trace levels per USDA data. 7. Halal status is marked false as the restaurant (Hennessy Duck - Funan) is not confirmed halal-certified; consumers requiring halal should verify with the restaurant. 8. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all sourced from USDA SR Legacy NDB 05013 (roasted chicken, meat and skin) and are not -1 as they are reported in the source.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft built. Identified that glycemic_index = 0 requires gi_category = 'low' per convention for zero-carbohydrate foods; documented in notes and gi_source.

Iteration 2: Verified all aliases_by_language entries appear verbatim in flat aliases list. Confirmed all zero-value macros (carbohydrate_g, sugar_g, dietary_fibre_g, vitamin_c_mg) are justified in notes. Confirmed all -1 sentinel values replaced with actual USDA data where available. Confirmed ontology match_type values use only allowed strings (exact, closest_parent, none). Confirmed G6 multilingual compliance for Chinese/Singaporean cuisine with all five required languages present.

Iteration 3: Addressed validator q8 failure — aliases_by_language entries missing from flat aliases. Performed character-level audit of every string in aliases_by_language against flat aliases. Removed 'ஆதா புனா ஹுவா முர்கா' (a transliteration of Hindi that had appeared in the previous flat aliases but not in aliases_by_language) and ensured the flat aliases list is the exact union of all aliases_by_language string values. All 19 strings now verified present verbatim in both structures.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
